pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 8 to 17 to target the new JDK.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile against Java 17 APIs and bytecode; keeping version 8 would result in incompatible class files or missing API errors on the target runtime.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports Java 17 compilation.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the compiler plugin may fail to compile Java 17 source code or generate invalid bytecode for the target JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Switch compiler configuration from separate source/target to the unified release flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'release' flag is the standard way to compile for a specific Java version in modern toolchains, ensuring consistent module path and API usage; while source/target might still work, release is required for proper multi-release JAR handling and strict API compliance in Java 17+.
